Mid-Level AssociateView Job ↗
2026-03-26
New York City, New York · Banking · 2020–2022 · 2 elite matches
Show job description
The Finance group in New York is seeking a candidate with four to six years (i.e., class of 2022-2020) of Digital Infrastructure experience, with data center financing experience a must. The candidate will also have a strong finance background.. High level of academic achievement and solid law firm experience preferred. The starting base salary for this position is expected to be between $310,000 to $390,000 annually. Actual salary will be determined based on skills, experience (to the extent relevant) and other job-related factors, consistent with applicable law. This position may be eligible for a discretionary year-end bonus.

Mid-Level AssociateView Job ↗
2026-03-26
New York City, New York · Banking · 2020–2022 · 2 elite matches
Show job description
The Finance group in New York is seeking a candidate with four to six years of Finance experience (i.e., class of 2022-2020). Expertise in various types of secured lending transactions and familiarity with drafting and negotiating security interest opinions would be ideal. Candidate would be expected to develop an expertise in advising clients on UCC issues, with an emphasis on Article 9 Secured Transactions and Article 8 Investment Securities matters. The work primarily consists of reviewing security documentation, advising clients on personal property security interest issues and structures and reviewing security interest opinions. Candidate would also be expected to keep abreast of any upcoming changes to the UCC and related laws and to help with the Firm’s knowledge management materials in this field. Candidate would support Finance attorneys as well as attorneys in various other transactional and non-transactional practice areas. High level of academic achievement and solid law fi

San Francisco, California · Litigation, Data Privacy · 2020–2021 · 1 elite match
Show job description
San Francisco, California Job Description Position Summary: Ideal candidates will be problem solvers, self-motivated, and have extensive first-hand proficiency with counseling businesses on major global and U.S. data protection legal frameworks, the behind-the-scenes workings of the Internet, AI systems, and privacy and regulatory compliance, in addition to superior writing and communication skills, comfort with ambiguity, and a proven record of providing exemplary client service. This is a partner track position. Ideal applicants will have 5 – 6 years of experience focusing on regulatory investigations or litigation involving consumer protection issues (State AG, FTC, and other regulatory agencies), and/or be well-versed in defending data privacy and security class actions (e.g., Stored Communications Act, wiretapping claims, claims involving allegedly unfair and deceptive trade practices, and data breach class actions). If you aspire to be the best, this is where you belong. About the Practice Group Latham & Watkins is seeking a talented mid-level (3rd-5th Year) associate to join its market-leading Fintech & Financial Services practice. Cadwalader, Wickersham & Taft Signed Client 1 role · 3 matches · best 179
Fund Finance Associate (3-6+ Years) - New York, NY OfficeView Job ↗
2026-03-26
New York City, New York · Banking, Corporate · 2020–2023 · 3 elite matches
Show job description
Cadwalader is seeking associates with 3-6 years of relevant experience for its Fund Finance practice. Qualified candidates will have experience in syndicated lending, commercial lending, leverage finance, fund formation, CLOs, asset-based lending, NAV financings, or acquisition financings. Candidates must possess excellent academic credentials and solid law firm legal experience. Selected candidates will get extensive interaction with preeminent bank, asset manager and lending clients. The anticipated annual base salary range for this position is $260,000 to $420,000. The actual base salary offered will depend on a variety of factors, including without limitation, the qualifications of the applicant, years of relevant experience, and, if applicable, the location from which the applicant will be performing the job.

Greenberg Traurig has an excellent opportunity for an M&A Associate to join the Corporate Practice of our Miami office. We offer competitive compensation and an excellent benefits package. The ideal candidate will have a minimum of four years of significant experience in corporate transactions with an emphasis on public and private M&A, including private equity. Candidates must have a strong working knowledge of acquisition agreements and the other documentation used in complex M&A and private equity transactions and proficiency in drafting the same. This position requires a candidate with strong interpersonal skills, a high degree of maturity, and a proven willingness to accept significant responsibility and manage a challenging workload within a fast-paced environment. Strong academic credentials and writing skills are essential. Nelson Mullins law firm is looking for a corporate associate to join its Affordable Housing Team. As an AmLaw100 firm, this will offer a unique opportunity to a lateral associate who wants to be part of an expanding team. This opportunity is open to multiple large markets. As a Nelson Mullins associate, you’ll be joining the ranks of over 1,000 attorneys and policy advisors. Want to know more about Nelson Mullins? Check out our Firm Culture here. All candidates are asked to submit a resume, deal summary, and a law school transcript. You have at least 1 years of meaningful experience in affordable housing. You possess excellent academic credentials and strong writing, analytical, and communications skills and are a member of the bar in a state in which Nelson Mullins has an office. With over 240 attorneys and eight office locations in Texas and Louisiana, Kean Miller serves as legal counsel to the people and industries that drive the energy-belt economy. From our offices in Houston, The Woodlands, Lake Charles, Lafayette, downtown Baton Rouge, Bluebonnet-Baton Rouge, New Orleans and Shreveport, Fortune 500 companies, national businesses, and local companies rely on Kean Miller for efficient legal services and practical advice, always provided in a cost-efficient manner. In the courtroom and the boardroom, Kean Miller attorneys create unique solutions, pragmatic strategies, and unparalleled value that allow our clients to perform at the highest level. Kean Miller is seeking a Litigation Attorney (Partnership Track Attorney or Reduced Hour Staff Attorney) in our Houston office to join our Offshore Energy and Marine Litigation practice. Hunton seeks an attorney with at least four years of project finance experience to join the Energy and Infrastructure team in a full-time partnership-track associate position in our New York office. Prior experience at a national or international law firm strongly preferred. Candidates must possess excellent oral and written communication skills, demonstrate initiative and potential for leadership, and have the judgment and confidence to work independently with clients and colleagues. New York bar membership is preferred, and in any event required within one year of joining the firm. EEO/drug-free workplace/E-Verify participant/Veteran/Disability. At the time of posting, the expected salary range for this position for full-time on-track associates is $310,000 to $435,000. Actual salary offers may vary based on a number of factors, including but not limited to, education, training, skill sets, licensure and certifications, practice area, experience, internal equity, and market data. Job Description: Cooley is a leading global law firm with offices across the US, Europe and Asia. Our clients, ranging from entrepreneurs to emerging companies to Fortune 50 corporations, partner with us on transformative deals, complex IP and regulatory matters and high-stakes litigation where innovation meets the law. As a firm we constantly strive to deliver the highest quality work and superior service on every matter entrusted to us. Our lawyers are organized into two departments—Business and Litigation—which are further organized into practice groups. Given our uncompromising commitment to the quality of work that we produce, remaining on, if not defining, the leading edge of the law in a business and legal environment that is evolving daily, and working efficiently and cost-effectively, we are intently focused on enabling our practitioners and practice groups to the greatest extent possible. Job Description: Cooley is a leading global law firm with offices across the US, Europe and Asia. Our clients, ranging from entrepreneurs to emerging companies to Fortune 50 corporations, partner with us on transformative deals, complex IP and regulatory matters and high-stakes litigation where innovation meets the law. As a firm we constantly strive to deliver the highest quality work and superior service on every matter entrusted to us. Our lawyers are organized into two departments—Business and Litigation—which are further organized into practice groups. Given our uncompromising commitment to the quality of work that we produce, remaining on, if not defining, the leading edge of the law in a business and legal environment that is evolving daily, and working efficiently and cost-effectively, we are intently focused on enabling our practitioners and practice groups to the greatest extent possible. Show job description
Founded in 2003, Kanner & Pintaluga is a NLJ500 and Mid-Market Pro 50 law firm that has recovered over $1 billion for property damage and personal injury clients nationwide. With nearly 100 lawyers and more than 30 offices throughout the Central and Southeastern United States, our primary goal is to achieve the most favorable outcome for our clients, who have the absolute right to receive the maximum compensation for their damages. POSITION SUMMARY: Kanner & Pintaluga, a dynamic and rapidly growing law firm, handles auto accidents, premise liability, medical malpractice, wrongful death cases, nursing home abuse and first-party home and business property damage throughout the State of Florida. We are seeking an experienced Plaintiff Bad Faith Attorney to join our Litigation team in Boca Raton, Florida. The ideal candidate will have significant experience in first-party property insurance and/or personal injury bad faith litigation, with the ability to identify, develop, and preserve

Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher 1 role · 3 matches · best 177
Labor & Employment (OC)View Job ↗
2026-03-26
Orange County, California · Labor & Employment · 2017–2021 · 3 elite matches
Show job description
We are seeking an associate in law school class years 2017 through 2021 to join the labor and employment team. The practice is primarily employer-side litigation, with some advice and investigations work as well. Litigation is mostly class and representative actions, both wage and hour and discrimination, and experience in the class actions space would be helpful. Three years+ in a peer law firm in the employment space would likely have requisite experience. The annual compensation range for this position is between $310,000 and $435,000. The salary offered within this range will depend upon qualifications and other operational considerations. Benefits offered for this position include health care; retirement benefits; paid days off, including sick time, and vacation time; parental leave; basic life insurance; Flexible Spending Accounts; as well as discretionary, performance-based bonuses.

REQUIRED EXPERIENCE AND SKILL SET * Drafting pleadings, workplace policies, and discovery * Drafting and arguing motions, and conducting administrative hearings * Taking and defending depositions * Conducting employee trainings * Providing day-to-day advice and counseling to employers QUALIFICATIONS * Juris Doctor (JD) from an accredited law school * Must be currently licensed to practice law in the State of California or eligible for admission without examination. We are seeking a commercial real estate finance associate with 4 or more years of experience representing commercial and investment banks, insurance companies, debt funds and/or other regulated and non-regulated lenders in the financing of commercial properties. District of Columbia · Energy, Government · ≤2025 · 6 elite matches
Show job description
Work with Spiegel & McDiarmid LLP partners on a wide range of legal matters including federal and state administrative law, public utility law, and climate and energy policy. Work directly with the firm’s clients to assess client needs and present best-suited legal strategies. Advocate for clients’ interests in a variety of settings, including state and federal administrative proceedings, utility rate cases, settlement negotiations, and meetings with agency officials, legislators, and counterparties. Prepare a wide range of documents relevant to the practice of public utility and energy law, including legal memos and briefs on climate and energy policy matters, comments on policy proposals and rulemakings by state and federal energy/environmental regulators, and legislative proposals. Prepare and review energy-related transactional documents, including memos and briefs on climate, energy, and utility policy issues between utility companies and customers. Conduct legal research on compl

Corporate – M&A/Private Equity (DE)View Job ↗
2026-03-26
Denver, Colorado · Corporate · 2021–2024 · 1 elite match
Show job description
We are seeking associates in law school class years 2021 to 2024 to join our M&A and Private Equity teams. Associates will be expected to advise corporate clients and private equity funds in connection with acquisitions, dispositions, minority investments, other similar transactions and general corporate matters. Candidates must have significant experience handling M&A transactions, a good understanding of corporate law and market terms and substantial experience in drafting transaction documents and effective deal management. Driven personality, business development-oriented mindset, and ability to take on high level of responsibilities and work with autonomy are key. The role is expected to have significant direct client contact and will offer a great opportunity for entrepreneurial associates to develop relationships with clients and manage matters with a high degree of independence. The annual compensation range for this position is $235,000 and $365,000. The salary offered within

Litigation Associate (Bankruptcy/Creditors’ Rights Litigation) - Washington, D.C.View Job ↗
2026-03-26
District of Columbia · Bankruptcy · 2022–2024 · 1 elite match
Show job description
Caplin & Drysdale is seeking a second to fourth-year associate to join our Bankruptcy/Creditors’ Rights practice group in our Washington, DC office. Our bankruptcy litigation practice is regularly retained in Chapter 11 bankruptcy cases to analyze and resolve high-profile, complex and cutting-edge disputes. Our practice encompasses the full range of issues potentially affecting creditors in bankruptcy proceedings. Applicants should have between 1-3 years of litigation experience, preferably with prior law firm or clerkship experience. Bankruptcy experience is not required but candidates with exposure to bankruptcy and/or creditors’ rights will receive additional consideration. All applicants must have exceptionally strong academic credentials and be in the top of their class from top tier law schools. Our litigation group works closely together and is looking for associates that are effective communicators that want to be part of a close-knit team. We offer competitive market salarie

Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom 1 role · 1 match · best 161
Washington, D.C. - Political Law Junior AssociateView Job ↗
2026-03-26
District of Columbia · Government · 2023–2025 · 1 elite match
Show job description
Skadden’s Political Law Compliance and Investigations Group is growing and seeking two junior associates (Class of 2025-2023) to join its Washington, DC office. This is a unique and exciting opportunity to join a top tier political law practice. Skadden’s Political Law Compliance and Investigations Group is strictly nonpartisan and does not represent political candidates or parties. Successful candidates must have a background in political law, exceptional legal research, writing and communications skills and a commitment to providing high quality service to clients. The position is best suited for candidates with a law firm background. The starting base salary for this Washington, DC position is expected to be between $225,000-$260,000 annually. Actual salary will be determined based on skills, experience (to the extent relevant) and other job-related factors, consistent with applicable law. This position may be eligible for a discretionary year-end bonus.

Corporate Mid-Level – Private CreditView Job ↗
2026-03-26
New York City, New York · Corporate · ≤2023 · 6 elite matches
Show job description
We are seeking attorneys for associate positions in Cahill Gordon & Reindel’s New York office for our Private Credit practice group. Candidates should have at least three years of experience in corporate work, with substantial exposure to private credit transactions including with direct lenders, private credit funds, and other institutional investors and investment banks in corporate finance transactions, leveraged buyouts, acquisition financings and recapitalization transactions. Preferred candidates will have experience in structuring and executing traditional first lien, unitranche, stretch senior, second lien and subordinated loan transactions, structured equity investments, including non-traditional preferred stock financings, and equity co-investments. Candidates must have excellent oral and written communication skills, a strong academic background and be admitted to the New York Bar. Show job description
Who We Are MoFo is a destination law firm for talented, ambitious attorneys who value collaboration, teamwork, and commitment to excellence. We are known around the world not only for delivering innovative yet practical client service but also for serving as an incubator for our top-notch talent. MoFo consistently receives top-tier rankings from leading directories and publications, speaking to our legal capabilities, dedication to creating an inclusive workplace, and commitment to our communities. Our Real Estate Practice Group MoFo has one of the leading real estate practices in the United States, Asia, and Europe. We provide innovative and practical legal advice to a broad range of real estate industry participants in all types of real estate transactions, including acquisition, development, financing, ownership, leasing, and operation. NYC Mid-level/Senior Corporate AssociateView Job ↗
2026-03-26
New York City, New York · Corporate · 2020–2021 · 5 elite matches
Show job description
Our Capital Markets & Corporate Department seeks an Associate with 5-6 years of corporate law firm experience, including some or all of the following: private equity, mergers & acquisitions, venture capital, joint ventures, general corporate and commercial agreements. This position offers the opportunity for significant responsibility and client contact. Successful candidates should possess excellent drafting and analytical skills, great attention to detail, high level of organization, strong verbal and interpersonal skills, and the ability to manage multiple projects and people, at times across offices. Superior academic credentials and admission to the New York Bar are required. Large or mid-size law firm experience preferred. We currently expect 3 days in-office, including Wednesdays. The salary range that we expect to pay for this position is a minimum of $285,000 and a maximum of $305,000 annually. The actual pay wage may vary based on experience or other relevant factors

Brown Rudnick Signed Client 1 role · 1 match · best 161
Bankruptcy & Corporate Restructuring AssociateView Job ↗
2026-03-26
New York City, New York · Bankruptcy · 2021–2023 · 1 elite match
Show job description
Bankruptcy & Corporate Restructuring Associate Dispute Resolution & Restructuring Brown Rudnick seeks a talented 3rd to 5th year associate to join the Bankruptcy & Corporate Restructuring group in New York. The ideal candidate will have restructuring experience including Chapter 11 cases, representing debtors and creditors in all areas of corporate restructurings and bankruptcy proceedings. Candidates should be capable of handling and running multiple significant matters at once and should be able to work collaboratively within a team. Our Dispute Resolution & Restructuring Department represents a diverse range of clients across high-stakes, complex matters in a fast-paced and collaborative environment. The team includes high-performing, creative, and experienced lawyers with deep expertise in Chapter 11 reorganizations, out-of-court workouts, catastrophic corporate loss, mass tort, liquidations, fixed charge and administrative receiverships, restructured credits and securitization

MG+M The Law Firm (MG+M) is seeking to add an experienced associate attorney in our Boston office to support our New England asbestos practice group. This is a hybrid role. MG+M is a nationally renowned litigation law firm concentrating on complex civil matters. The firm’s expertise is diverse but focused in the areas of toxic tort, complex product liability, local government, general liability, and commercial litigation. We approach litigation as a unified team, integrating our efforts with those of our clients to advance their interests wherever they do business. Our goal is always to deliver optimal results at the best possible value. Essential Duties and Responsibilities: Handle local and national accounts throughout New England states. Independently manage a litigation caseload. Conduct depositions. Prepare witnesses for deposition and trial. Review and analyze discovery requests. Prepare and respond to discovery responses and pleadings. Prepare court filings and service to counse

JOB TITLE: ATTORNEY DEPARTMENT: MASS TORT REPORTS TO: BONNIE RICKERT FLSA STATUS: EXEMPT ROLE SUMMARY Hilliard Law’s mission for our clients is clear - the “Relentless Pursuit of Justice.” We strive to find talent that pursues excellence for our clients. We believe our success is a result of our people, and we’re looking for individuals who bring their experience, dedication, problem-solving skills, and creativity to further our mission. Hilliard Law is seeking a Mass Tort Attorney to join our dynamic legal team in a fast-paced, high-impact environment. This role is responsible for managing and litigating mass tort cases, advocating on behalf of clients who have been harmed by defective products, pharmaceuticals, or environmental hazards. The attorney will play a key role in case strategy, client communication, and all phases of litigation. Dorsey & Whitney LLP is seeking a litigation associate with three to five years of experience to join our New York office. The ideal candidate will be interested in working on a variety of commercial disputes, along with some exposure to intellectual property and employment litigation matters. This associate will have the opportunity to work in all phases of litigation, from pleadings through discovery, dispositive motion practice, trial, and appeal. We anticipate that this position will involve responsibility over cases in federal and state court and in arbitration, and we expect that it will provide in-court experience and opportunities to work on motion and trial practice. Overview Orrick currently has an excellent opportunity for a Litigation & IP Innovation Attorney. This position could be based in any of our U.S. offices and consideration given for 100% remote US locations. The Innovation Attorney will support the Firm’s innovation efforts to improve the delivery of legal services through the trifecta of people, process, and technology, specifically focusing on our Litigation and IP business units. This position is part of our Innovation team and collaborates across offices, practice groups, and departments to execute on projects and initiatives. The role reports to the Managing Director of Practice Innovation and works in close collaboration with all members and leadership of the Litigation and IP business units. Job Description: Holland & Knight LLP is seeking an International Trade Group Associate for its New York, NY office. Job duties: Advise clients on regulatory aspects of inbound and outbound investments and assist with trade due diligence, utilizing experience in cross-border mergers and acquisitions. Counsel clients on trade-related restructuring of foreign manufacturing processes, supply chains, and broader business operations to mitigate exposure to U.S. duties and tariffs, utilizing experience of managing and guiding cross-border legal teams across EMEA and LATAM regions during large-scale business spin-offs and reorganizations. Commercial Real Estate Finance AssociateView Job ↗
2026-03-26
New York City, New York · Banking, Real Estate · 2019–2022 · 2 elite matches
Show job description
We are seeking an associate class of 2022 to 2019 with commercial real estate finance experience to join our Global Finance group. Dechert is recognized as one of the few firms — if not the only firm — that maintains a premier commercial real estate capital markets practice concentrating in loan origination, loan acquisition and disposition, securitization, rating agency representation, servicing, workouts/restructuring and bankruptcy matters. Our practice is top ranked across industry publications, including Chambers and Partners and The Legal 500 US. Salary is $310,000+ commensurate with class year and experience. WE ARE LOOKING FOR: * Hands on experience with commercial real estate loans and mezzanine loans. * Experience as a key member of deal teams, including and running key aspects of transactions from due diligence through closing. * Large law firm experience strongly preferred.

WILSON ELSER [https://www.wilsonelser.com/] is a leading defense litigation law firm with more than 1400 attorneys in 46 offices throughout the United States. Founded in 1978, we rank among the top 100 law firms identified by The American Lawyer and 36 in the National Law Journal’s survey of the nation’s largest law firms. Our firm is committed to attracting and retaining professionals who value each other and the service we provide by embracing Teamwork, Collaboration, Client Service, and Innovation. If you are a motivated professional looking for a long-term fit where you can grow in a role, and will be valued and empowered, then we invite you to apply to our GENERAL LIABILITY ASSOCIATE ATTORNEY position in our PHILADELPHIA Office. _THIS POSITION OFFERS A FLEXIBLE, HYBRID WORKING ARRANGEMENT._ THE POSITION KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: * Independently manage a litigation caseload, working cases from beginning to end * Work closely with other attorneys and Partners on legal projects

DLA Piper is, at its core, bold, exceptional, collaborative and supportive. Our people are the backbone, heart and soul of our firm. Wherever you are in your professional journey, DLA Piper is a place you can engage in meaningful work and grow your career. Let’s see what we can achieve. Together. Summary DLA Piper is seeking a lawyer with a minimum of five years’ experience handling commercial leases (specifically including office, industrial and retail) to join its Real Estate Group. Solar leasing experience is valued but not required. This position is open to our Atlanta, Boston, California, Chicago, Miami, New York, Reston, Texas and Washington, DC offices. Cipriani & Werner is a multidisciplinary law firm. We have long been recognized as a firm that seeks to understand the needs and interests of our clients. From that point forward, we strive to deliver results that are expected by our clients. The Cyber Group out of our Philadelphia, PA Office is looking for an Associate to join our growing team. Responsibilities include, but are not limited to: * Conduct legal research and analysis on various legal issues. * Draft legal documents. * Collaborate with senior attorneys on cases. * Maintain billable hours as necessary. * Manage client relationships and a caseload with supervision. Position Requirements: * Must have a JD degree from an ABA-approved law school and maintain an active license in good standing. * 5-10 years of litigation experience is required; experience in Cyber Security is preferred. * Trial experience preferred. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office 365, ProLaw, Excel, and e-document management software preferred

ABOUT ASHURST: Ashurst is a leading global law firm with a determination to go beyond the ordinary by outpacing change. We have a community of 4800+ people across over 30 offices globally and are a trusted adviser to top corporates, financial institutions and governments. What sets us apart is our ambition for our people and our clients. TEAM OVERVIEW: Ashurst’ global loans / banking practice is recognised by its clients to be one of the most innovative and solution orientated legal practices in the city today. As the challenges to our clients have increased over the last few years we have stayed true to our core offering of legal technical excellence wrapped in commercial awareness. This has enabled Ashurst to continue to fulfil the role of being a Trusted Advisor to our clients and strengthened our close relationships. JOB OVERVIEW: Join a rapidly expanding U.S. team in 2026 as part of our premier Global Loans practice. This role offers the opportunity to support a sophistic

Nelson Mullins law firm is looking to add a MID-LEVEL CIVIL LITIGATION ASSOCIATE to its product liability team in the LOS ANGELES office. As an AmLaw100 firm, this is a unique opportunity to join an expanding practice that offers a range of litigation services to clients in a variety of industries. You’ll be joining an outgoing and fast paced team that handles all aspects of litigation. Many of the attorneys have worked in-house for major motor vehicle manufacturers and they draw on the resources of the Firm to provide national and international clients counsel in areas including employment, environmental, intellectual property, and technology matters. Our LA office is positioned twenty minutes from downtown Los Angeles and twenty minutes from Redondo Beach. Show job description
Job Description: The Century City office of Sidley Austin LLP is seeking associates to join its M&A and Private Equity Group. Candidates should have at least four years of experience and should have experience with private equity and leveraged buyout transactions. Responsibilities include drafting and negotiating acquisition agreements, supervising and training junior associates and coordinating legal specialists working on the deal team. Salaries vary by location and are based on numerous factors, including, but not limited to, the relevant market, skills, experience, and education of the selected candidate. The target salary range for this role is below. Our compensation package also includes bonus eligibility and a comprehensive benefits program. Benefits information can be found here [https://www.sidleycareers.com/en/northamerica/why-sidley?tab=associate-life]. TARGET SALARY RANGE $250,000 - $415,000 Sidley Austin LLP is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Job Requirements:

Show job description
Overview Orrick currently has an excellent opportunity for a Managing Attorney in the Washington, DC office with consideration given for 100% remote US locations. Under the supervision of the Director of the Court Services Department, the DC Managing Attorney will primarily be responsible for advising case teams on federal and state court procedure and practice, calculating procedural deadlines in Compulaw and supervising others doing the same, and providing litigation related support to case teams. Responsibilities Advise attorneys on a wide range of procedural issues based on technical knowledge of court rules and experience. Review drafts of filings for compliance with court rules and judge specific requirements. Handle electronic filing of documents in state and federal courts. Perform detailed analysis and legal research on court rules, procedure, and case histories, as needed, using Westlaw, Lexis, Bloomberg Law, and similar resources. Calendar the entry of litigation events in C

New York City, New York · Real Estate · 2020–2023 · 7 elite matches
Show job description
The New York office of Blank Rome LLP has a special opportunity for an accomplished Real Estate Leasing Associate with at least 3-6 years of experience handling sophisticated commercial leasing transactions, particularly within the high-end retail market and luxury real estate. The ideal candidate can demonstrate ability to think strategically, self-starter and is business minded. Candidates must be self-motivated, personable team players who are comfortable directly interfacing with clients and leading matters as appropriate. The role offers exposure to marquee clients, complex deal structures, and close collaboration with leading real estate partners on matters shaping premier assets in New York and nationally. Outstanding academic and professional credentials are needed to join this exciting, energetic, fast-paced team. Experience in a large law firm setting is preferred. Candidates must be admitted to practice in New York.

2026-03-26
San Antonio, Texas · Litigation · ≤2023 · 1 elite match
Show job description
At Mayer LLP, we believe in doing law differently — with collaboration, innovation and determination at the core. Founded by a team of experienced, entrepreneurial attorneys, we’ve built a firm where client interests come first and teamwork drives results. With a Southwest regional footprint spanning Texas, New Mexico and Arkansas and a breadth of practice areas including corporate & securities, litigation, real estate, tax, labor & employment, healthcare and immigration, we serve clients ranging from start-ups to Fortune 500 companies across industries. If you thrive in a dynamic, growth-minded environment where your contribution directly matters and you’ll work alongside skilled professionals focused on achieving business-driven legal solutions, Mayer is where you can make an impact. New York City, New York · ERISA · 2022–2024 · 1 elite match
Show job description
Kelley Drye & Warren LLP seeks an associate with 2-4 years of law firm experience to join its Employee Benefits and Executive Compensation (EBEC) practice. The EBEC group is tight knit and values a collaborative work environment. The group currently consists of three partners, and the practice is broad-based with corporate and non-profit clients in an array of industries. A successful associate will have executive compensation (409A), qualified plan (ERISA), and transactional experience. Responsibilities will include: * Assisting clients in developing, implementing, administering, and terminating qualified and non-qualified retirement plans, health and welfare benefit plans, and other compensatory arrangements, including stock option plans, restricted stock, equity-based awards, omnibus equity plans, cash bonus plans, employee stock purchase plans, executive retirement plans, and other deferred compensation arrangements. * Handling the benefits aspects of mergers, acquisitions,

Greenberg Traurig has an excellent opportunity for an M&A Associate to join the Corporate Practice of our Chicago office. We offer competitive compensation and an excellent benefits package. The ideal candidate will have a minimum of four years of significant experience in corporate transactions with an emphasis on public and private M&A, including private equity. Candidates must have a strong working knowledge of acquisition agreements and the other documentation used in complex M&A and private equity transactions and proficiency in drafting the same. This position requires a candidate with strong interpersonal skills, a high degree of maturity, and a proven willingness to accept significant responsibility and manage a challenging workload within a fast-paced environment. Strong academic credentials and writing skills are essential. Associate LitigationView Job ↗
2026-03-26
New York City, New York · Litigation · 2021–2023 · 21 elite matches
Show job description
Our Commercial Litigation Practice specializes in representing clients in high-stakes business disputes across a wide range of industries. We provide expert legal services in areas such as contract disputes, class actions, business torts, shareholder and partnership disputes, antitrust litigation and intellectual property litigation. Our team handles all phases of litigation, from pre-litigation ccounseling and strategic planning to trial and appellate proceedings. By leveraging our deep legal expertise, global reach and strategic insights, we help clients navigate complex legal challenges, protect their business interests, and achieve favorable outcomes through both court proceedings and alternative dispute resolution methods. Roles at this level focus on handling legal tasks that require strong understanding of applicable laws related to Disputes matters. They represent clients (under the supervision of partners) in preliminary hearings and depositions alongside senior colleagues, pr

Los Angeles, California · Litigation · 2021–2024 · 2 elite matches
Show job description
Our Commercial Litigation Practice specializes in representing clients in high-stakes business disputes across a wide range of industries. We provide expert legal services in areas such as contract disputes, class actions, business torts, shareholder and partnership disputes, antitrust litigation and intellectual property litigation. Our team handles all phases of litigation, from pre-litigation ccounseling and strategic planning to trial and appellate proceedings. By leveraging our deep legal expertise, global reach and strategic insights, we help clients navigate complex legal challenges, protect their business interests, and achieve favorable outcomes through both court proceedings and alternative dispute resolution methods. We are currently looking for associates with 2-5 years of Commercial Litigation experience to join our Los Angeles ofice. Show job description
Cadwalader’s Capital Markets Department is seeking an associate with 3 to 6 years of experience to join our Charlotte office. Candidates should have experience in drafting, reviewing, and negotiating transaction documents related to commercial mortgage loan securitizations, including pooling and servicing agreements, offering documents, mortgage loan purchase agreements, certificate purchase agreements, placement agency agreements, legal opinions and related contracts. Candidate should have experience managing the closing process for complex transactions. Candidates must demonstrate strong attention to detail, excellent communication skills, teamwork, a solid work ethic, and a proactive sense of ownership over their responsibilities. The anticipated annual base salary range for this position is $260,000 to $390,000. The New York City office of FBT Gibbons is seeking a Product, Tort & Insurance Litigation Associate. FBT Gibbons LLP is a national, full-service law firm formed through the combination of Frost Brown Todd and Gibbons, bringing together more than 800 attorneys across 25 offices. Our attorneys offer comprehensive legal solutions to clients ranging from Fortune 500 companies and global multinationals to startups and nonprofits. FBT Gibbons distinguishes itself as a dynamic, forward-moving firm with a coast-to-coast footprint and a reputation for excellence across multiple practice areas and industries. Our reputation is further reinforced by its numerous Chambers and Best Lawyers ranked practices and attorneys. We are a people‑first firm recognized for our award-winning inclusive culture and a strong commitment to community and pro bono work. Chapman and Cutler LLP is seeking to hire an Associate to join the corporate trust group of its Asset Securitization Department. The new hire can sit in any of our U.S. office locations: Charlotte, Chicago, New York City, Salt Lake City, San Francisco, or Washington, D.C. The successful candidate will have a minimum of three years of experience in corporate trust, loan agency and/or other finance transactional work at a large or mid-size law firm. Job Description At K&L Gates, we are looking for smart, imaginative and hard-working people with diverse backgrounds, experiences and ideas to join us. Perhaps our search for talented visionaries and your search for important and impactful work lead to the same place. We are seeking an experienced midlevel associate to join our Corporate M&A team. This role may be based in Boston, Charleston, Charlotte, Miami, Nashville, Pittsburgh, Portland, Raleigh, or Seattle. The ideal candidate will have three to five years of Corporate M&A experience, with private equity transaction experience preferred. Key qualifications include: • Strong M&A experience, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating merger agreements, stock purchase agreements, asset purchase agreements, letters of intent, and related documents. Private equity experience is a plus. • Experience with partnership agreements, joint ventures, and limited liability company operating agreements. • Meaningful responsibility for manag

Candidates must have law firm experience and previous experience advocating for clients. PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITES: Conduct legal research and analysis to support litigation matters. Draft pleadings, motions, briefs, and other legal documents with attention to detail. Prepare discovery requests and responses. 2026-03-26
Los Angeles, California · Labor & Employment · 2020–2023 · 2 elite matches
Show job description
The Woodland Hills office of Fisher Phillips seeks employment litigation associates with 3–6 years of experience. Ideal candidates will be comfortable drafting and responding to discovery and initial pleadings, drafting and arguing motions, as well as taking and defending depositions. Significant experience in labor and employment law and handling California wage-and-hour class action claims and PAGA cases is preferred, but candidates with strong litigation experience and a demonstrated interest in labor and employment law will be considered. Candidates must have excellent academic credentials, strong writing and analytical skills, and the ability to manage multiple projects in a fast-paced environment. Successful candidates will be self-starters with the ability to work independently and as part of a team. Candidates must be admitted in California and hold a J.D. from an ABA-accredited law school. We are accepting submissions from search firms for this position. To apply, please submi

Show job description
King & Spalding seeks an associate for its Employee Benefits and Executive Compensation practice in Atlanta, Chicago, New York or Washington D.C. Qualified candidates will have solid academic credentials from a top-tier law school, and a minimum of 2 years’ law firm experience in employee benefits and executive compensation, including experience with employee benefit issues in the context of mergers and acquisitions and financings, executive employment, retention and separation agreements, and the design, implementation and administration of equity plans and nonqualified deferred compensation arrangements. Experience with ERISA plan investments and/or with the design, implementation, and administration of qualified retirement plans and health and welfare programs is preferred. Show job description
Type of Experience: King & Spalding seeks a mid-level associate for its underwriting and structured finance and capital markets team in New York. Ideal candidates should have experience advising financial institutions on securities offerings under the Securities Act of 1933, including representing issuers and underwriters in registered and exempt transactions. Familiarity with disclosure requirements, offering documentation, and compliance matters under the 1933 Act is essential. Candidates with general capital markets or leveraged finance backgrounds and a strong interest in securities law are encouraged to apply. The practice provides for a mix of esoteric asset-backed securities offerings, registered equity underwriting offerings, public-company M&A, at-the-market offerings, and special purpose acquisition companies. Atlanta, Georgia · Real Estate · 2020–2023 · 1 elite match
Show job description
Taft Law is seeking a mid-level associate for our Real Estate Group with at least three or more years of experience representing clients in sophisticated commercial real estate transactions. The ideal candidate will possess top academic credentials, strong writing and analytical skills, business acumen, and significant experience with all manner of commercial transactional real estate work, including acquisitions, dispositions, financings, joint ventures, development work, and leasing. Large law firm experience is also strongly preferred Taft offers competitive compensation and a comprehensive benefit package, including medical, dental, vision, life, long term disability, flexible spending, 401K, Paid Time Off, paid holidays, bonus and more. Interested candidates should apply here. Show job description
Greenberg Traurig (GT), a global law firm, has an exciting full-time employment opportunity for Corporate Funds Associates in the New York office. We offer competitive compensation and an excellent benefits package. GT is consistently among the top firms on the Am Law Global 100; the Associate will have the opportunity to work with clients, large and small, from variety of industries across the country. Candidates should have 3 to 5 years of experience to join a national-caliber private funds practice. Qualified candidates will have graduated from a nationally recognized law school in the top 10% of their class and must have a corporate background. The ideal candidate will have experience in investment management or funds work. All candidates must have strong writing capabilities, be strategically and analytically minded, and be able to work both independently as well as part of a cohesive team and be highly motivated with a strong work ethic. Los Angeles, California · Litigation · 2019–2024 · 3 elite matches
Show job description
Firm Summary White & Case is an elite global law ﬁrm serving leading companies, ﬁnancial institutions and governments worldwide. Our long history as an international ﬁrm means we are perfectly placed to help our clients resolve their most complex legal challenges wherever they may be. With lawyers operating from more than 40 locations, working around the world, we have invested heavily in building a high-quality full-service practice competing at the top of the market. We are distinguished by our on-the-ground presence in the world’s key ﬁnancial markets and our strengths in handling complex cross-border work. It’s not just about our global network of ofices; it’s the global interconnectedness of the Firm that our people, and our clients, value most. We work well together across geographic and practice boundaries. It’s one of the reasons we attract and retain cross-border work. Our lawyers are globally minded, enterprising, collaborative and committed to excellence. Our people represen

Show job description
Join our collaborative team of corporate lawyers working with exciting biotech and tech companies, guiding and supporting them from inception, through funding, and on to the IPO process and life as public company, or sale. We are looking for corporate attorneys with three to four years of experience for an associate or of counsel position with background and interest in the dynamic world of life sciences and tech. Desired expertise includes an ability to advise companies through the start-up and venture capital phase, IPOs and equity capital markets, and public company reporting. The position will be based in Palo Alto with a hybrid schedule. You will gain deep and broad experience working directly with clients bringing world-changing products and technologies to market. Compensation and Benefits Salary range of $260,000 – $310,000, based on year classification. Discretionary merit bonuses may also be awarded. Lathrop GPM is actively seeking a stak attorney with 2-4 years’ experience in product liability defense to join our growing Tort Insurance & Environmental practice group in our Chicago okice. Candidates should have a J.D. from an accredited law school, an active Illinois law license (Missouri law license a plus) and be in good standing, strong academic credentials, strong research, writing, and analytical abilities, excellent client service skills, outstanding interpersonal and problem- solving skills, and the ability to work in a collaborative environment. Deposition and court appearance experience are ideal but not required. The current anticipated salary range is $110,000 to $120,000. Actual initial salary may be above or below the identiﬁed ranges and will be based on relevant skills, training, experience, and other job-related factors consistent with applicable law. About Ashurst: Ashurst is a leading global law firm with a history spanning 200 years, and a clear strategy for our future growth. Our in-depth understanding of our clients and commitment to providing exceptional standards of service have seen us become a trusted adviser to local and global corporates, financial institutions and governments in all areas of commercial law. To find out more please visit here . Team overview: Ashurst is one of the most experienced and respected law firms active in the global infrastructure/projects market. We are consistently ranked Tier 1 in both Chambers and Legal 500 and our lawyers win praise for their deep knowledge of the industry sectors our clients operate in, such as energy and resources, transport, social infrastructure, construction, sports and telecommunications. Berliner Cohen, LLP, is a highly respected full-service business law firm in Northern California. If you are a self-starter, this is a great opportunity to grow your legal career at a community-focused, well-established firm. 2026-03-27

Show job description
DLA Piper is, at its core, bold, exceptional, collaborative and supportive. Our people are the backbone, heart and soul of our firm. Wherever you are in your professional journey, DLA Piper is a place you can engage in meaningful work and grow your career. Let’s see what we can achieve. Together. Summary DLA Piper is seeking a finance associate with a focus on Fund Finance and Private Credit transactions to join its Leveraged Finance subgroup. Candidates should have a minimum of four years of relevant experience in fund finance, private credit, leveraged finance, debt capital markets, and/or structured finance. Our clients are primarily lead arrangers, direct lenders, sub line lenders and NAV lenders, but we also work with borrowers, issuers and corporates. This position is open to our New York, Los Angeles, Austin, Baltimore, Boston, Chicago, Dallas, Houston, Miami, Northern Virginia, Palo Alto, Philadelphia, Phoenix, Raleigh, San Diego, San Franciso, Seattle, Short Hills or Washingto

ATLANTA, GEORGIA: [https://gallowaylawfirm.com/offices/atlanta/] Galloway is seeking an associate attorney to join our Atlanta office and take on a meaningful role within a fast paced and growing practice that includes a mix of civil litigation and business-related work. This position is ideal for a practicing attorney who takes ownership of their work, exercises sound judgment, and wants to build a long-term career as part of a collaborative and expanding team. Our Atlanta practice handles a broad range of matters including commercial litigation, construction disputes, insurance defense, premises liability, trucking and transportation cases, and representation of business and development clients, with opportunities to assist on transactional and advisory work as the practice continues to grow. At Levenfeld Pearlstein, we know that everything starts with the right people. Our mission to provide exceptional service to our clients depends upon the talent and commitment of each person that makes up our firm. If you’re looking for a different way of practicing law, find out if LP could be the right place for you. Position: Levenfeld Pearlstein (LP) is currently seeking an associate with 6-8 years of sophisticated commercial real estate experience to join its growing, collaborative, and highly credentialed Real Estate practice group. About our practice: LP’s Real Estate group handles all types of real estate matters across the country, including acquisitions and dispositions, joint venture transactions, real estate development, manufactured housing, commercial leasing — including significant experience with retail leasing — real estate tax structuring, and debt and equity financing. The New York, New York office of FBT Gibbons is seeking a Business Litigation Associate. FBT Gibbons LLP is a national, full-service law firm formed through the combination of Frost Brown Todd and Gibbons, bringing together more than 800 attorneys across 25 offices. Our attorneys offer comprehensive legal solutions to clients ranging from Fortune 500 companies and global multinationals to startups and nonprofits. FBT Gibbons distinguishes itself as a dynamic, forward-moving firm with a coast-to-coast footprint and a reputation for excellence across multiple practice areas and industries. Our reputation is further reinforced by its numerous Chambers and Best Lawyers ranked practices and attorneys. We are a people‑first firm recognized for our award-winning inclusive culture and a strong commitment to community and pro bono work. Show job description
Greenberg Traurig, an international Am Law Global 100 ﬁrm, is seeking a Real Estate Litigation Associate in the New York ohice. This is a unique opportunity for a motivated individual from a law ﬁrm or in-house to join a growing, nationally acclaimed real estate litigation practice that advises sophisticated, top-tier clients on high-proﬁle litigations nationwide. The Associate will gain meaningful experience, direct client exposure, and a path to advancement in a shareholder-track position. Ideal candidates must be collaborative, have strong academic credentials, and 3-8 years of real estate and commercial lease litigation experience. Outstanding writing, communication, client-service, and teamwork skills are essential. Candidates must be admitted to the NY bar. Apply today by submitting your resume, writing sample, and law school transcript (an unohicial transcript is acceptable). Submissions from search ﬁrms will only be accepted through our web portal for third party submissions. F

As it proudly celebrates 80 years of service and legal excellence, Bennett, Bricklin & Saltzburg LLC is seeking an experienced attorney to join its Workers’ Compensation Department. The ideal candidate will have at least ﬁve (5) years of experience in defense of workers’ compensation and be admitted to practice in Pennsylvania. The ﬁrm operates under a hybrid work schedule, allowing attorneys to work remotely for a portion of the workweek. Show job description
About Cullen and Dykman LLP Cullen and Dykman LLP is a full-service law ﬁrm that has been serving clients since 1850. With a long history of excellence and innovation, we are committed to providing outstanding legal services across diverse practice areas. Our collaborative culture, ﬂexible work arrangements, and dedication to professional growth make Cullen and Dykman LLP a dynamic place to build your career. What You’ll Do Join our growing Construction Litigation Group in Manhattan, where you will represent clients in complex matters involving construction disputes, NYS Labor Law defense, and construction defect/property damage claims. We are seeking both junior and senior associates with diverse backgrounds to strengthen our team. Key Responsibilities: Handle depositions, court appearances, and motion practice in state and federal courts. About F3 Law At F3 Law, we believe that a culture of inclusion and belonging, one of embracing and respecting each other’s diherences, strengthens our ability to serve our clients and communities. We are committed to fostering a workplace where individuals of all backgrounds feel valued and appreciated. By bringing together diherent perspectives and lived experiences, we create better solutions and a more dynamic, thoughtful, and ehective team. F3 Law provides essential legal support to schools, colleges and other public entities, helping them navigate complex legal issues to fulﬁll their 110 of 174 mission of serving students and communities. Summary F3 Law provides legal support to the public schools that serve our children and communities. We have an opening for a Special Education Litigation Attorney to join our ﬁrm. Position Summary Rimon, P.C. is an international law ﬁrm that is growing rapidly and seeks a Conﬂicts Attorney to provide conﬂicts analysis for new business and new hire requests ﬁrmwide. This is a completely remote opportunity that will allow you to be able to perform each job duty and give you a steady work-life balance inside our growing team. This position supports the Conﬂicts Department (the “Department”) and the Ohice of General Ccounsel in responding to new business requests submitted ﬁrm wide, including requests related to existing and potential new clients as well as requests related to new hires, external publications, and requests for proposal. By utilizing a solid understanding of basic litigation concepts, business organizations and ﬁnancing concepts, the Conﬂicts Attorney (“Attorney”) is called upon to make and communicate dihicult decisions, handle multiple complex situations, and produce high quality work while under pressure. The primary responsibility of the Attorney

Stradley Ronon Stevens & Young, LLP is seeking a Litigation Associate with 5+ years of experience to join our team of nationally recognized litigators in Philadelphia. Our associates have a hands-on role in interesting, complex litigation in a wide variety of substantive matters in state and federal courts, private arbitration, and before governmental agencies. This is an excellent opportunity for an associate looking to deepen their litigation skills as part of a collaborative, high-performing team. We invest in our associates through immersive training, practical experience, and dedicated mentorship to support long-term growth. Post-Clerkship AssociateView Job ↗
2026-03-28
District of Columbia New York City, New York · Litigation · 2019–2024 · 7 elite matches
Show job description
New York and Washington D.C. Ohices We are seeking post-clerkship associates to join the Litigation Department and the Appeals & Strategic Motions Practice Group. Willkie highly values the experience judicial clerks bring to the ﬁrm. Former clerks play an integral role across our practice, and our team includes former Supreme Court, federal court of appeals, and district court clerks. Attorneys with clerkship experience are in strong demand across the ﬁrm’s litigation platform. The ﬁrm is actively recruiting judicial clerks who are eager to work closely with experienced practitioners, beneﬁt from meaningful mentorship, and enhance our growing practice. While the ﬁrm considers judicial clerks year-round, those interested in joining the ﬁrm in the fall are encouraged to apply during the preceding winter. The ﬁrm’s judicial clerk bonuses are competitive. Clerks may submit an application here. Applications should include a resume, transcript, and writing sample. Modiﬁed Job Listings (11)

San Diego, California · Litigation · 2021–2023 · 2 elite matches
Show job description
Our San Diego ohice has an immediate opening for a Litigation Associate with 3-5 years of commercial litigation experience. We are looking for candidates with experience in both State and Federal Courts litigating complex matters, including antitrust, class action and other sophisticated issues. Candidates must be self-starters with excellent research and writing skills. This position ohers the opportunity for client interaction, on-your-feet court experience and direct involvement in every aspect of litigation including discovery, depositions, brief writing and trial. Candidates must have excellent academic credentials and prior litigation experience. California bar admission required. Marshall Dennehey, a leading Am Law 200 civil defense law ﬁrm with over sixty years of continued service to our corporate, insurance, and individual clients, is seeking an Associate Attorney for its Melville, New York ohice with at least one (1) year of experience to join their growing Mass Torts & Industry-wide Litigation team. Compensation range: $100,000 - $150,000 The posted compensation range for this position is based on several legitimate, non-discriminatory factors set by the Firm. Marshall Dennehey is committed to ensuring equal pay opportunities for equal work regardless of gender, race, or any other category protected by federal, state, or local pay equity laws. Marshall Dennehey seeks to hire attorneys who want to ﬁnd their “forever home.” We welcome candidates who desire to advance within the ﬁrm while assisting others to do the same. Job Description: Summary: The Trademark Stah Attorney supports the Firm’s Technology and Life Sciences group with a primary focus on trademark clearance and prosecution, branding initiatives, enforcement and licensing, UDRP and copyright registration. Duties and Responsibilities: Advise clients about branding, trademark strategy, prosecution, enforcement, licensing and other related matters. Manage domestic and foreign prosecution for multiple clients, including drafting trademark/service mark applications, ahidavits of use, maintenance and renewal documentation and correspondence to clients. Draft responses to ohice actions issued by the USPTO. Provide clearance analysis for new trademarks. Prepare draft cease and desist demands and required follow-up. Prepare draft pleadings and other ﬁlings for opposition proceedings and appeals.
